name: unsupported_connector_razorpay_then_pivot_to_sheet
notes: Steady-state (flow=plan_kicked is terminal — the paced post-profile beats are done, so an owner-initiated connector request reaches the integration agent directly rather than the journey orphan re-offer, which would pitch Shopify and never name the connector actually asked for). The owner asks to connect Razorpay — a VT-205 registry placeholder OUTSIDE OWNER_VISIBLE_CONNECTOR_IDS ({shopify, google_sheet}). integration_agent.start_oauth returns a fail-closed status:'unsupported' envelope ('This connector isn't supported yet — Viabe Team currently connects Shopify and Google Sheets') and the system prompt says to report an unbuilt connector plainly, never promising a walkthrough. Step 1 must name Razorpay honestly as not-yet-supported (no fabricated link/connection); step 2 the owner pivots to a REAL connector (Google Sheets) and the agent proceeds with that honestly, carrying no leftover Razorpay 'connected' claim. Distinct from unsupported_connector_woocommerce_direct.json (whose second beat enumerates 'what CAN you connect') — this one exercises the PIVOT to a supported connector after the unsupported ask. Setup: `setup --onboarded --flow plan_kicked` then `script <tenant> unsupported_connector_razorpay_mid_sheets_flow.json`.

steps.message: while we're at it, can you also hook up Razorpay so you can see my payment settlements?
steps.note: Ask for an unbuilt connector by name. Expect an honest 'not supported yet' that names Razorpay — no fabricated link, no silent drop.

steps.message: okay no worries — then just connect my Google Sheet instead, that's where my customer list is
steps.note: Owner pivots to a REAL, supported connector. Expect the agent to engage the Google Sheets connect (offer / authorize link / picker guidance) — never a leftover fabricated Razorpay claim.
